mentions.raw_name: Four unnamed Peasebury men (the cannibals, including a defiant serjeant and a weeping youth)
Bryen Farring (dead; king's squire, Godry's kin)
Godry Farring's cousin (likely Bryen Farring; taunted by serjeant)
House Lannister (holding true lord of Karhold captive)
House Frey (enemies at Winterfell, per Arnolf)
House Norrey (mentioned as hill clan sharing old gods)
Queen's men (R'hllor devotees among Stannis's host)
Night's Watch (two riders in all black among the arriving party)
Iron Bank of Braavos (Tycho Nestoris's institution)
Northmen / northern lords (faction within Stannis's host, sit opposite southerners)
Southerners / southron lords (faction within Stannis's host)
Hill clans (Wulls, Norreys, Flints — worship old gods)
R'hllor / the Lord of Light / "Red Rahloo" (queen's men's god; burning sacrifices; nightfires; "the night is dark and full of terrors"; prayers for melting snow)
Old Gods of the North (weirwood with red eyes on the island; northmen say these gods are watching; "wroth of the old gods")
Drowned God (Asha's god; "God of my fathers… in your watery halls beneath the waves"; she recalls Iron Islands priests slitting thralls' throats and giving bodies to the sea)
Ironborn (Asha, Theon, Tristifer, and companions; finger dancing; iron price; kraken identity)
Northmen (big ruddy men with thick beards, clad in fur and iron; garrons and bear-paws for snow; ice-fishing expertise)
Southerners (gaunt, hollow-cheeked; not adapted to northern conditions)
Tyroshi (referenced for fashion comparison — Tycho's hat)
Tycho Nestoris's hat (brimless tower of soft fabric, three stacked cylinders)
Paean of praise for R'hllor (queen's men sing during burning; words inaudible over screams)
mentions.section: Raw Entity List > In-world Texts & Songs

mentions.raw_name: King's blood (Suggs says "There's power in king's blood, the red woman used to say"; Asha believed to have it as Balon's daughter)
Fire visions (Stannis stares into beacon fire "to search the flames for answers"; Will Foxglove says "His Grace has seen victory in his fires")
Weirwood face (slitted red eyes and bloody mouth on the island weirwood; red sap that looks like frozen blood)
The unnatural storm (nineteen days of unrelenting blizzard; sun, moon, and stars absent; snow burying the village)
R'hllor sacrifice ritual (burning living men as offering to stop the storm; prayers and chants)
War of the Five Kings (ongoing context; march on Winterfell; Bolton occupation)
Ironborn raids on the North (Moat Cailin, Deepwood Motte, Torrhen's Square, centuries of reaving along the stony shore)
Theon's actions at Winterfell (referenced as one of Asha's crimes to answer for)
King (Stannis Baratheon; "His Grace," "His Good Grace the king")
Castellan of Karhold (Arnolf Karstark's actual title, not lord)
Serjeant (rank of the defiant Peasebury cannibal)
